events.title: Japan tsunami alerts downgraded following powerful earthquake off northern coast – as it happened
events.description: People in affected areas are still urged to evacuate after quake registering 7.7 magnitude Australian officials in Japan are urgently following up on the tsunami warning off the northeastern coast of the island of Honshu. The Australian government said: We stand ready to provide consular assistance. Australians in need of emergency consular assistance should contact the Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade’s 24-hour Consular Emergency Centre on 1300 555 135, or +61 2 6261 3305 (if calling from overseas). Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Japan tsunami alerts downgraded following powerful earthquake off northern coast – as it happened

events.title: Air Canada temporarily suspends some flights to New York and other locations
events.description: Separately, Spirit Airlines reportedly seeks emergency US government funds as Iran war keeps aviation fuel costs high Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox Air Canada has announced a temporary suspension of flights from Toronto and Montreal to New York ’s John F Kennedy airport, citing rising fuel prices. The move comes amid growing concerns that airlines worldwide may scale back services as aviation fuel costs climb in the wake of the US and Israel’s ongoing war with Iran, which entered a fragile ceasefire earlier in April. Although Iran announced on Friday that the strait of Hormuz had reopened, helping ease oil prices, fuel costs remain significantly elevated after weeks of disruption. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Air Canada temporarily suspends some flights to New York and other locations

events.title: Eight children killed in US domestic violence shooting
events.description: A man shot dead eight children -- seven of them his own -- early Sunday in the southern US state of Louisiana in an incident of domestic violence that spanned three locations, police said. The early morning massacre at a gray house in the city of Shreveport was the deadliest mass shooting in the United States in more than two years, according to data from the Gun Violence Archive. Two women were shot and seriously injured, including the mother of some of the slain children.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Eight children killed in US domestic violence shooting

events.title: More than 38,000 women and girls killed in Gaza war, UN gender equality agency reports
events.description: The war in Gaza has inflicted a far higher toll on women and girls than in previous conflicts in the Palestinian enclave, with more than 38,000 killed by Israeli air bombardment and land military operations since Hamas-led terror attacks in Israel sparked the war in October 2023, UN Women said on Friday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 More than 38,000 women and girls killed in Gaza war, UN gender equality agency reports

events.title: Massive 7.7-magnitude earthquake hits off Japanese coast
events.description: A strong earthquake erupted off the coast of the nation of Japan, the Associated Press reported, noting the Pacific Tsunami Warning Center indicated the tsunami threat "has now passed."
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Massive 7.7-magnitude earthquake hits off Japanese coast

events.title: Japan on high alert for 'huge' second quake after issuing tsunami warning
events.description: Japan's meteorological agency warns another, stronger earthquake may hit in the next week,
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Japan on high alert for 'huge' second quake after issuing tsunami warning

events.title: Israel army says established 'Yellow Line' in Lebanon, as in Gaza
events.description: Israel's military said Saturday it had established a "Yellow Line" demarcation in southern Lebanon, similar to one separating its forces from Hamas-held areas in Gaza, adding it had hit a "terrorist cell" operating near its troops along the line. Israel and Lebanon agreed to a 10-day ceasefire on Thursday in order to negotiate an end to six weeks of war between Israel and the Iran-backed group Hezbollah.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Israel army says established 'Yellow Line' in Lebanon, as in Gaza

events.title: Deadly Russian strikes on Ukrainians ‘simply trying to live their lives’ must stop, says senior UN official
events.description: The latest wave of Russian attacks on Ukrainian civilians in Dnipro, Kyiv and Odesa, which killed and injured scores of civilians, was roundly condemned by Matthias Schmale, the UN Humanitarian Coordinator in the country.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Deadly Russian strikes on Ukrainians ‘simply trying to live their lives’ must stop, says senior UN official

events.title: As the ‘world court’ turns 80, Guterres says law must prevail over force
events.description: The world is at a “moment of crisis” and countries must reaffirm commitment to international law amid rising violations and geopolitical tensions, UN Secretary-General António Guterres said at an event on Friday to commemorate the 80th anniversary of the International Court of Justice (ICJ).

events.title: Japan issues tsunami alert after strong earthquake
events.description: The 7.4-magnitude quake off northern Iwate prefecture could be felt as far as Tokyo. Residents have been urged to evacuate northern coastal regions ahead of a tsunami.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Japan issues tsunami alert after strong earthquake

events.title: 'Hierarchy of sovereignty': Israel claims authority over 'which Lebanese areas are inhabitable'
events.description: Carys Garland is pleased to welcome Nadim Houry, Executive Director of the Arab Reform Initiative (ARI). According to Houry, the “Yellow Line doctrine” is not a security measure, but rather a military tool for territorial control. Drawing on recent developments in Gaza, Syria, and Lebanon, he argues that this doctrine operates as a legal façade for expanding occupation under the language of self-defence. All the while, these practices actually undermine both ceasefire agreements and established principles of international law, threatening Lebanon's sovereignty.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 'Hierarchy of sovereignty': Israel claims authority over 'which Lebanese areas are inhabitable'

events.title: Moscow-born gunman dead after Kyiv shooting rampage leaves at least 6 dead, 14 wounded: Zelenskyy
events.description: Kyiv supermarket shooting leaves at least 6 dead, several wounded after a Russian gunman took hostages and opened fire, according to Ukrainian officials.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Moscow-born gunman dead after Kyiv shooting rampage leaves at least 6 dead, 14 wounded: Zelenskyy

events.title: NDIS infiltrated by organised crime gangs using intimidation and threats of violence against Australians
events.description: Review recommends better use of NDIS data to identify repeat rorters and a requirement for providers to register with the government Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Organised crime gangs are using the national disability insurance scheme to launder money, earn income and hide assets, law enforcement officials have warned parliament, seriously undermining probity in the $50bn program . The Australian Criminal Intelligence Commission (ACIC) has told a review into NDIS integrity that criminals are paying cash kickbacks to participants and their families, and sometimes resorting to intimidation and threats of physical violence towards vulnerable people to rip off taxpayers. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 NDIS infiltrated by organised crime gangs using intimidation and threats of violence against Australians

events.title: World News in Brief: Myanmar amnesty, rising needs in Afghanistan, another power loss at Ukraine nuclear plant
events.description: Authorities in Myanmar released the country’s ousted president from prison on Friday, along with some 4,000 other people, as part of an amnesty to mark the traditional New Year festival.

events.title: South Koreans breathe sighs of relief as escaped wolf is returned to zoo safely
events.description: Nine-day search for two-year-old Neukgu gripped nation and sparked safety concerns for animal and public The internet in South Korea erupted in celebration as a two-year-old wolf that escaped from a zoo was captured safely after a nine-day search that had gripped the nation and made the animal a national celebrity. The male wolf, named Neukgu, burrowed out of his enclosure at the O-World zoo in Daejeon on 8 April. Animal rights activists questioned whether the wolf could survive outside the zoo and also worried he might be killed during capture, something that happened to a puma that escaped from the same zoo in 2018. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 South Koreans breathe sighs of relief as escaped wolf is returned to zoo safely

events.title: ‘Israel never talked me into the war with Iran,’ Trump says
events.description: US president suggests attacking Iran was his own decision as war struggles to garner public support amid energy crisis.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘Israel never talked me into the war with Iran,’ Trump says

events.title: Time running out on development goals as finance dries up, UN warns
events.description: Rising conflicts, the climate crisis and shrinking development finance are putting growing pressure on the poorest and most vulnerable countries – pushing development goals further off track.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Time running out on development goals as finance dries up, UN warns

events.title: Police identify suspect who killed eight kids, most believed to be his own, after multi scene domestic rampage
events.description: Shamar Elkins is accused of killing eight children and shooting two women in a Shreveport domestic violence rampage that spanned multiple locations.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Police identify suspect who killed eight kids, most believed to be his own, after multi scene domestic rampage

events.title: Slain Iranian nuclear scientists raises alarm over uranium, expertise reaching black market
events.description: The killing of Iranian nuclear scientists in U.S.-Israeli strikes raises fears over nuclear proliferation as experts warn of weakened material controls.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Slain Iranian nuclear scientists raises alarm over uranium, expertise reaching black market

events.title: New Zealand declares state of emergency in Wellington as floods hit
events.description: Footage online shows vehicles submerged, trees uprooted and houses hit by landslides.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ New Zealand declares state of emergency in Wellington as floods hit

events.title: Ceasefire at risk after US seizes Iranian ship, Iran shuns peace talks
events.description: A ceasefire between the United States and Iran appeared in jeopardy on Monday after the US said it had seized an Iranian cargo ship that tried to run its blockade and Tehran vowed to retaliate, refusing for now to join new peace talks. Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmaeil Baghaei said Washington has shown it was "not serious" about pursuing the diplomatic process ​and Tehran would not change ‌its clearly stated demands, adding that it did not believe in deadlines or ultimatums when safeguarding national interests. FRANCE 24's International Affairs Editor Angela Diffley tells us more.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ceasefire at risk after US seizes Iranian ship, Iran shuns peace talks

events.title: State of emergency declared in New Zealand after flash flooding
events.description: A state of emergency has been declared in New Zealand after heavy rain triggered flash flooding.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ State of emergency declared in New Zealand after flash flooding

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 20 April: Uncertainty grows in the Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Tensions have escalated in the Strait of Hormuz amid renewed security incidents, including reported attacks on vessels and a US interception of an Iranian cargo ship. It's unclear if more talks will take place in the coming day between Washington and Tehran in Islamabad. In Lebanon, a 10-day ceasefire with Israel continues to hold, with thousands returning to the south despite widespread destruction. A UN peacekeeper was also killed over the weekend – likely from Hezbollah fire. Stay with us for live updates. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 20 April: Uncertainty grows in the Strait of Hormuz

events.title: At least 25 killed in firecracker factory blast in India
events.description: Mostly female workers have been killed in the blast, which occurred in Tamil Nadu state.

events.title: Sudan war enters fourth year as child hunger crisis deepens
events.description: Sudan’s war has displaced millions, leaving parts of the country facing famine. Aid agencies warn children are bearing the brunt as food shortages worsen and humanitarian funding declines.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Sudan war enters fourth year as child hunger crisis deepens

events.title: Iranian American woman arrested in Los Angeles for alleged arms trafficking
events.description: Federal prosecutor says woman is suspected of dealing weapons to Africa on behalf of Iranian government A California woman was arrested at Los Angeles international airport after allegedly trafficking weapons on behalf of the Iranian government to contacts in Africa, including Sudan. Shamim Mafi, 44, of Woodland Hills was detained on Saturday night by federal agents, according to the top federal prosecutor in Los Angeles. Continue reading...

events.title: US and Mexican officials assigned to cartel case killed in car accident
events.description: Director of state investigation agency among those killed in Chihuahua in operation to destroy clandestine drug labs Two United States officials and another two Mexican officials assigned to combat drug cartel operations died in a car accident in the northern Mexican state of Chihuahua on Sunday, a US embassy spokesperson said. The Mexican officials were the director of the state’s investigation agency and an officer, state authorities said, adding that they were on an operation to destroy clandestine laboratories in the municipality of Morelos. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 US and Mexican officials assigned to cartel case killed in car accident

events.title: US strike on alleged drug boat in Caribbean kills 3
events.description: Trump has said that the US is in "armed conflict" with cartels, as its military campaign has killed at least 180 people since last year amid criticism over its legality.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US strike on alleged drug boat in Caribbean kills 3

events.title: At least six killed in Kyiv shooting
events.description: At least six people have been killed after a gunman opened fire in Kyiv.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 At least six killed in Kyiv shooting

events.title: UN chief strongly condemns killing of another peacekeeper in southern Lebanon
events.description: A UN peacekeeper has been killed and three others injured after a UNIFIL patrol came under fire in southern Lebanon, the mission said on Saturday.

events.title: Iran-US ceasefire talks: Nuclear program remains sticking point
events.description: Differences over ​Iran's nuclear programme ​still remain, a senior Iranian source told Reuters ​on ‌Monday, ⁠as Iran and ‌the United States try ⁠to reach a lasting peace with ​the two-week ‌ceasefire ending soon. The senior Iranian source ‌said that Tehran's "defensive capabilities", ​including its missile programme, are not open to negotiation ​with the ​United States.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran-US ceasefire talks: Nuclear program remains sticking point

events.title: Shockwaves of Middle East war reach Caribbean as food prices soar
events.description: Six weeks since war erupted in the Middle East, the shockwaves have spread to the Caribbean region, already pushed to the brink, amid fears of a looming El Niño-linked climate disaster.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Shockwaves of Middle East war reach Caribbean as food prices soar

events.title: 2 US Army soldiers in Alaska injured in bear attack during training exercise
events.description: Two Army soldiers were attacked by a brown bear in Alaska during a training exercise at at Joint Base Elmendorf-Richardson's Arctic Valley training area.

events.title: At least 21 killed as bus crashes into Kashmir gorge
events.description: The overcrowded bus skidded as it navigated a sharp curve in the mountainous region.

events.title: 'Situation is dire' for displaced people in Lebanon, journalist says
events.description: Less than a week after a fragile ceasefire was announced in Lebanon, a senior Anglophone journalist in the country has spoken to FRANCE 24 about the devastation people are seeing as they start to return home. Thousands of homes have been destroyed in the conflict, and more than a million people displaced. Iva Kovic-Chahine, the head of L'Orient Today, also told us how people are closely following the peace process between Iran and the US, as Lebanon has become something of a card played by Tehran. She spoke to us in Perspective.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 'Situation is dire' for displaced people in Lebanon, journalist says

events.title: Ukraine police chief resigns after officers reportedly fled Kyiv shooting
events.description: A police chief in Ukraine has resigned after officers reportedly fled the scene of a shooting in Kyiv which killed seven people.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Ukraine police chief resigns after officers reportedly fled Kyiv shooting

events.title: What to know as ceasefire in the Iran war hangs in the balance
events.description: The ceasefire in the Iran war hangs in the balance as Pakistan’s capital stands prepared for possible new talks between Tehran and Washington
events.ticker_label: 🪖 What to know as ceasefire in the Iran war hangs in the balance

events.title: Gender, geography and powerbroking in play in race for next UN chief
events.description: The choice of the tenth UN Secretary-General, who will take office in January 2027, could shape global diplomacy, the response to crises across the world and the direction of the multilateral system for the next decade.

events.title: Several University of Iowa students wounded in downtown shooting after fight erupts near campus
events.description: A shooting in downtown Iowa City near the University of Iowa's campus wounded multiple people, including students, and police say the suspect remains at large Sunday.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Several University of Iowa students wounded in downtown shooting after fight erupts near campus

events.title: NYC teen shot dead on Queens basketball court as bystanders filmed; police searching for gunman
events.description: A 15-year-old boy, Jaden Pierre, was shot and killed on a basketball court in Queens' Roy Wilkins Park after being attacked by a group of teens, the NYPD reported.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 NYC teen shot dead on Queens basketball court as bystanders filmed; police searching for gunman

events.title: Carney says Canada’s strong economic ties to US are ‘weakness’ to be corrected
events.description: Prime minister details efforts to attract investment and sign trade deals with other countries in 10-minute video address Canada ’s strong economic ties to the United States were once a strength but are now a weakness that must be corrected, the country’s prime minister has warned In a 10-minute video address, Mark Carney spoke about his government’s efforts to strengthen the Canadian economy by attracting new investments and signing trade deals with other countries. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Carney says Canada’s strong economic ties to US are ‘weakness’ to be corrected

events.title: Macron meets Tusk to discuss possible Polish role in French nuclear deterrence
events.description: French President Emmanuel Macron and Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk met Monday at a summit in the Polish city of Gdansk to discuss defence cooperation between the two countries. As Europe ramps up its defence capabilities amid growing threats from Russia and US President Donald Trump's questioning of NATO, both sides have said that Poland could contribute to French nuclear deterrence.

events.title: Israel army warns Lebanese against returning to south
events.description: Israel's military on Monday warned Lebanese civilians against returning to dozens of villages in southern Lebanon, claiming Hezbollah's activities in the area were violating a ceasefire agreement struck last week. Thousands of displaced residents have begun making their way back to parts of southern Lebanon since the truce between Israel and Lebanon took effect on Friday. FRANCE 24's Cyril Payen tells us more.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Israel army warns Lebanese against returning to south

events.title: Why lower fertility does not have to mean economic decline
events.description: Globally, most people say they want two or more children, but many are having only one, or none at all. According to a senior UN economist, fears of a demographic timebomb are unwarranted.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Why lower fertility does not have to mean economic decline

events.title: Man kills seven of his children, and an eighth child, in Louisiana mass shooting
events.description: Three boys and five girls, aged between three to 11, were killed in the shooting on Sunday, officials say.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Man kills seven of his children, and an eighth child, in Louisiana mass shooting

events.title: Minns doubles down on ‘rational’ anti-protest law despite NSW’s highest court ruling it unconstitutional
events.description: Greens criticise premier’s ‘extraordinary attack’ on judiciary and urge him to accept he ‘got it wrong’ Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The New South Wales premier has doubled down on an anti-protest law struck down in the state’s highest court last week, defending the legislation introduced by his government as “rational and proportionate”. But advocates for protesters charged at demonstrations restricted under the laws have criticised Chris Minns’ comments, calling them a “extraordinary attack” on the judiciary. Continue reading...

events.title: Scientists, authorities divided over fate of stranded whale in Baltic Sea
events.description: Timmy, a humpback whale, first became stuck off Germany's Baltic coast in late March. The animal’s plight has since gripped the public and raised questions about whether humans should intervene to save wild animals. While a rescue mission funded by two German millionaires is underway, marine biologists warn the whale has no chance of survival and are urging the public to reflect on humanity's role in the degradation of marine habitats. FRANCE 24's Environment Editor Valerie Dekimpe takes a closer look.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Scientists, authorities divided over fate of stranded whale in Baltic Sea

events.title: Sudan: Three quarters of women feel unsafe as war rages on
events.description: Across war-torn Sudan, women and girls “are telling a consistent story of continued experience of danger, and risks for gender-based violence” whether when fleeing to safety or arriving at displacement camps, a senior official with the UN reproductive and sexual health agency UNFPA said on Friday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Sudan: Three quarters of women feel unsafe as war rages on

events.title: More than half of Haitians continue to face food crisis
events.description: 5.8 million Haitians, or roughly 52 per cent of the population, are facing crisis levels of food insecurity, or worse. Of those, more than 1.8 million are dealing with emergency levels, which means they are exhausting their last assets and unable to meet even basic food needs.

events.title: UNESCO grants ‘enhanced protection’ to cultural heritage sites in the Middle East
events.description: Since the Middle East war started on 28 February, several sites of major cultural significance have come under attack in Israel, Iran and Lebanon. Ensuring their protection is the task of the UN agency for education, science and culture, UNESCO.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 UNESCO grants ‘enhanced protection’ to cultural heritage sites in the Middle East

events.title: UK chief rabbi says Jews targeted by ‘sustained campaign of violence and intimidation' after string of attacks
events.description: Police probe suspected Iranian proxy group allegedly behind a wave of arson attacks targeting Jewish sites in London, raising alarm across Europe.

events.title: Australia's most decorated veteran walks free on bail on war crimes charges related to Afghan deaths
events.description: Australia's most decorated living veteran Ben Roberts-Smith was released on bail after being charged with war crime murder over killings in Afghanistan.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Australia's most decorated veteran walks free on bail on war crimes charges related to Afghan deaths

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
events.description: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

events.title: Unpacking the 2026 Venice Biennale controversies and highlights
events.description: Russia's return to the international contemporary art exhibition, as well as the participation of Israel and the US, have sparked politicized backlash.

events.title: Alleged Irish cartel boss arrested in covert operation on organized crime charges after years-long manhunt
events.description: International fugitive Daniel Kinahan, alleged leader of Ireland's Kinahan cartel, was arrested in Dubai this week on organized crime charges.

events.title: Philippines, US and allies start military exercises testing 'real‑world' readiness
events.description: Philippine and US forces will carry out maritime strike drills on a remote Philippine island near Taiwan during annual exercises that started on Monday, which Manila's military chief said would ​test their readiness under "real-world ‌conditions". The April 20 to May 8 exercises known as "Balikatan", or "shoulder-to-shoulder", will be ⁠the largest yet in terms of participating countries, with Australia included again and Canada, France, New Zealand and Japan joining as active participants for the first ‌time.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Philippines, US and allies start military exercises testing 'real‑world' readiness

events.title: A top paramilitary commander defects to Sudan’s military as war enters 4th year
events.description: A high-profile paramilitary commander in Sudan has switched sides and joined the country’s army as the war enters a fourth year
events.ticker_label: 🪖 A top paramilitary commander defects to Sudan’s military as war enters 4th year

events.title: Japan warns of slightly increased risk of mega-quake after a 7.7-magnitude one
events.description: Japan has issued an advisory for northern coastal areas for an increased risk of a possible mega-quake following a 7.7-magnitude quake earlier Monday
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Japan warns of slightly increased risk of mega-quake after a 7.7-magnitude one

events.title: New food alert platform helps humanitarians combat hunger
events.description: The UN World Food Programme (WFP) released its next-generation platform on Thursday known as HungerMap Live, a digital monitoring and intelligence site that integrates food security data with predictive modelling to help fight hunger in more than 50 countries.

events.title: String of scientist deaths, vanishings fuels expert talks of shadow ops and silenced secrets: 'Very serious'
events.description: Nearly a dozen scientists have died or disappeared since 2023 under mysterious circumstances, prompting a federal response and expert speculation.
events.ticker_label: 💥 String of scientist deaths, vanishings fuels expert talks of shadow ops and silenced secrets: 'Very serious'

events.title: Ukraine police chief resigns after officers allegedly fled deadly shooting
events.description: Authorities say the officers have been suspended and an investigation into their actions is under way.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Ukraine police chief resigns after officers allegedly fled deadly shooting

events.title: Rat poison found in HiPP baby food jar in Austria, police say
events.description: The brand warned on Saturday that consuming the purées may be potentially "life-threatening".
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Rat poison found in HiPP baby food jar in Austria, police say

events.title: Several injured as fans clash with police in Paraguay’s Superclasico match
events.description: Olimpia vs Cerro Porteno derby abandoned after 29 minutes as police fire rubber bullets and tear gas at fans.

events.title: Fire destroys 1,000 ‘stilt’ homes in Malaysia’s Sabah, displacing thousands
events.description: Blaze struck a ‘water village’ that is home to some of Malaysia’s poorest residents A huge fire destroyed about 1,000 makeshift homes, many of them built on stilts over water, and displaced thousands of people in a coastal village in Malaysia’s Sabah state on Sunday, authorities said. The blaze broke out early on Sunday morning in a “water village” in Sandakan district in Sabah’s northeast, where some of Malaysia’s poorest residents, including indigenous and stateless communities, live in closely packed, wooden stilt houses. Continue reading...

events.title: Ukraine hits Russian Black Sea port city, 1 dead
events.description: Russian authorities said one man was killed in a Ukrainian drone raid on city of Tuapse. Meanwhile, Russia arrested a German woman in an alleged bomb plot. DW has the latest.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Ukraine hits Russian Black Sea port city, 1 dead

events.title: Iran war: Tehran vows response to US seizure of cargo ship
events.description: US Marines have seized an Iranian-flagged cargo ship near the Hormuz Strait, President Trump has said. Earlier, he threatened to destroy every bridge and power plant in Iran if no peace deal was reached.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war: Tehran vows response to US seizure of cargo ship

events.title: UN peacekeeping missions under strain as funding cuts and new threats grow
events.description: From election support in the Central African Republic to patrols in the disputed Abyei region, UN peacekeepers are operating in increasingly volatile environments – but shrinking resources and new threats, including drone warfare, are testing their ability to keep communities safe.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 UN peacekeeping missions under strain as funding cuts and new threats grow

events.title: Two US Embassy personnel killed in 'accident' in Mexico, ambassador says
events.description: Two U.S. Embassy personnel were killed in an "accident" in Mexico, according to U.S. Ambassador to Mexico Ronald Johnson. Details remain unclear.

events.title: Hezbollah ‘human shield’ strategy behind Lebanon ambush, bomb detonation - Macron drawn in
events.description: A French soldier was killed in a southern Lebanon ambush allegedly carried out by Hezbollah, straining the 10-day Israel-Lebanon ceasefire agreement.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Hezbollah ‘human shield’ strategy behind Lebanon ambush, bomb detonation - Macron drawn in

events.title: These were the best and worst places for air quality in 2021, new report shows
events.description: Air pollution spiked to unhealthy levels around the world in 2021, according to a new report.

events.title: North Korea launches ballistic missiles toward sea
events.description: South Korea's Joint Chiefs of Staff says the launches happened on Sunday morning from the North's eastern Sinpo area.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 North Korea launches ballistic missiles toward sea

events.title: Renowned physicist alarmed by 'unheard of' number of scientists dying or vanishing now on White House's radar
events.description: Multiple scientists linked to UFO research, nuclear labs and NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory have mysteriously vanished or died in recent years.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Renowned physicist alarmed by 'unheard of' number of scientists dying or vanishing now on White House's radar

events.title: Pope Leo says remarks about world being 'ravaged by a ​handful of tyrants' were not aimed at Trump: report
events.description: Pope Leo XIV said his remarks about the world being ravaged by tyrants were not directed at President Donald Trump amid their ongoing public exchange of words.

events.title: Ugandan military reports rescue of some 200 hostages from ADF militants in eastern DRC
events.description: Uganda's military has said that it and forces from the neighboring Democratic Republic of Congo freed some 200 hostages in eastern DRC in a joint operation against Islamist militants known as the ADF.

events.title: India's 'wells of death': Gravity-defying stuntmen become social media phenomenon
events.description: It looks almost impossible: cars and motorbikes racing at full speed inside towering vertical arenas in India. These dangerous stunt shows are known as the "wells of death", where drivers cling to the walls as gravity seems to disappear. The races attract large numbers of young Indians and have gone viral on social media. Our France 2 colleagues report, with FRANCE 24's Guillaume Gougeon.

events.title: UK police arrest 2 in connection with weekend arson attack on synagogue
events.description: British police have arrested two teenagers in connection with a weekend arson attack on a synagogue in northwest London as Jewish leaders express concern about a wave of recent incidents targeting their community

events.title: Record number of Rohingya refugees died at sea in 2025: UNHCR
events.description: In 2025, nearly 900 Rohingya refugees were reported missing or dead in the Andaman Sea and Bay of Bengal, making it the deadliest year on record in South and Southeast Asia, the UN refugee agency, UNHCR, said on Friday.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Record number of Rohingya refugees died at sea in 2025: UNHCR

events.title: Pennsylvania man accused of stealing over 100 sets of human remains appears in court in ‘horror movie’ case
events.description: Jonathan Gerlach, accused of stealing over 100 sets of human remains from cemeteries, reportedly waived his evidentiary hearing in Delaware County court.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Pennsylvania man accused of stealing over 100 sets of human remains appears in court in ‘horror movie’ case

events.title: The surreal facades of America's strip clubs
events.description: Some people travel the world in search of adventure, while others seek out natural wonders, cultural landmarks or culinary experiences. But French photographer François Prost was looking for something altogether different during his recent road trip across America: strip clubs.

events.title: Kenyan firm sacks more than 1,000 workers after losing Meta contract
events.description: Meta paused work with Sama last month after allegations about staff viewing private scenes filmed by smart glasses More than 1,000 low-paid workers in Kenya have been abruptly sacked by an outsourcing company contracted by Meta, in what activists said was a shocking move exposing the precariousness of tech jobs in the global south. Sama, a company based in Nairobi to which Meta outsourced content moderation and AI training work, announced on Thursday that the workers were being laid off after Meta terminated a contract. Continue reading...

events.title: Humanoid robots show rapid advances as they race past humans in Beijing half marathon
events.description: Winning robot runs faster time than Jacob Kiplimo’s world record More than 100 robots run in parallel tracks to avoid collisions with humans They can already carry the shopping, cook and clean. Now they can run and win half marathons. In perhaps the most unusual spectacle ever seen at the end of the 13.1-mile (21.1km) race, robots flew over the finish line ahead of the humans for the first time in Beijing on Sunday. And there wasn’t a bead of sweat in sight. Continue reading...

events.title: A humanoid robot sprints past the human half-marathon world record in Beijing race
events.description: The winner from Honor, a Chinese smartphone maker, completed the race in 50 minutes and 26 seconds, running faster than the human world record in a show of China's technological leaps.

events.title: How China's chip expansion puts pressure on global rivals

events.title: Robot beats human half-marathon world record
events.description: #China's technological developments were on full display, and at full speed, in the robot half-marathon in #Beijing on April 19. The winning robot beat the human half-marathon record by nearly seven minutes, finishing in 50 minutes and 26 seconds.

events.title: Billion-dollar tariff refund process kicks off in US

Also in this edition: oil prices surge again after Iran vows to block the Strait of Hormuz. The situation is causing severe disruptions to the supply of materials like plastic. Plus, a humanoid robot in China beats the human half-marathon world record.

events.title: Trump criticizes Spain amid Iran, NATO rift as PM Sanchez faces questions over political motives

events.title: Germany news: Timmy the stranded whale back on the move

events.title: 'Anatomy of a Cry': Crystal Murray on teen fame and going indie
events.description: French-American artist Crystal Murray rose to fame on social media, a decade before the term influencer was "a thing". She was already making alternative R'n'B and soul, which led her to get signed and release a first album in 2024. A couple of years ago, she moved to London to find her voice on her own terms and became an independent artist. She popped by arts24 to tell Marjorie Hache about her new EP "Anatomy of a Cry", which sees her already avant-garde style tinted with indie folk. They also discuss new releases by Massive Attack, the Foo Fighters and Fally Ipupa.

events.title: What to know about Timmy, the humpback whale that's sick and stranded in Baltic Sea
events.description: A humpback whale’s likely final days in the Baltic Sea have been livestreamed across the globe as multiple rescue efforts failed to coax it back into deeper waters while the marine mammal gets sicker and weaker

events.title: Weather tracker: hail covers parts of Tunisia and Algeria like snow
events.description: Accumulations of up to 3cm deep reported as severe thunderstorms also bring heavy downpours to central Italy Severe thunderstorms have affected the Mediterranean this week. On Monday, a surface low-pressure system in the Mediterranean in conjunction with an upper air cut-off low, led to thunderstorms over north Africa. Their intensity was aided by the hot precursor conditions. Algeria and Tunisia were notably affected by the thunderstorms, with some hail accumulation layers as a result. When so much hail forms, it starts to lay down sheets of hail, covering the ground like snow. Hail accumulations of up to 3cm were reported in Oum Ladjoul and Hammam Sokhna in Algeria, and there were hailstones of up to 3cm in diameter in Makthar, Tunisia. Thunderstorms continued in the region through the following day, with further hail accumulations, notably in Ouled Bousmir, Tunisia, where there was a layer about 2cm deep. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Weather tracker: hail covers parts of Tunisia and Algeria like snow

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 17 April: Israel-Lebanon ceasefire begins; Iran announces opening of Strait of Hormuz
events.description: A 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon came into effect at midnight in Beirut, offering a pause in hostilities after weeks of fighting. The UN Secretary-General has welcomed the agreement and urged all parties to respect it, expressing hope it could open the way for further negotiations. He also welcomed Iran’s announcement that the Strait of Hormuz will remain open to commercial vessels for the remainder of the ceasefire, calling it a step in the right direction. Stay with us for live updates. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 17 April: Israel-Lebanon ceasefire begins; Iran announces opening of Strait of Hormuz

events.title: Inside smoky shelters, a fast-paced, illegal card game has taken off in Solomon Islands
events.description: Growing numbers in the capital Honiara are playing the street card game Pass for a chance of a big payout, while risking big losses As the school day ends in Honiara, *Irene, a 43-year-old teacher in a floral dress with a yellow daisy in her bun, steps on to a minibus. After 10 minutes, Irene gets off the bus, walks down an alley, and enters a damp, smoky shelter. Plastic tables fill the space and playing cards are scattered on the floor. Irene has stopped by a hidden gambling table in a western suburb of Honiara to play Pass, a street card game gaining popularity in the Solomon Islands capital. Continue reading...

events.title: Consumer watchdog zeroes in on Woolworths’ allegedly fake discounts as it meets supermarket giant in court
events.description: Vinegar, Tim Tams and baby rice are among the products to be scrutinised in the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission’s case Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The consumer watchdog is back in court and taking on Australia’s largest supermarket chain, alleging Woolworths deliberately misled shoppers with fake discounts. The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission’s (ACCC) case against Woolworths begins in the federal court in Sydney on Tuesday, almost two months after hearings wrapped up in its very similar case against Coles . Continue reading...

events.title: Water NSW criticised for ‘appalling’ decision after hundreds of turtles left to die in wetlands
events.description: River ecologist says ‘classic bureaucratic tangle’ led to government agency stopping flows to Gwydir wetlands region in March Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A leading scientist has criticised an “appalling” New South Wales government agency decision to stop water flowing to wetlands in the state’s north-west, saying it was “absolutely crazy” that researchers had to scramble to save animals buried in drying mud. Guardian Australia reported on Saturday that turtles, waterbirds, frogs and sheep had died after Water NSW abruptly stopped flows to the Gwydir wetlands region near Moree in March. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Water NSW criticised for ‘appalling’ decision after hundreds of turtles left to die in wetlands

events.title: 'US military leverage a risky tactic': Bombs, military escalation will not bring Iran 'to the table'
events.description: Genie Godula is pleased to welcome Jim Townsend, Adjunct Senior Fellow in the CNAS Transatlantic Security Program and former Deputy Assistant Secretary of Defense (DASD) for European and NATO Policy. According to Townsend, US–Iran negotiations are shaped by tactical manoeuvering: a pre-negotiation ritual in which both sides position themselves strategically through public posturing and calculated obstruction. Ultimately, meaningful progress will depend on mediation, incremental trust-building, and the extension of fragile ceasefires.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 'US military leverage a risky tactic': Bombs, military escalation will not bring Iran 'to the table'

events.title: Arrests fuel fears among Madagascar’s gen Z protesters that new regime no better than one they overthrew
events.description: Jubilation is turning to disenchantment as young activists arrested after protest calling for election date to be set The arrest of several protesters in Madagascar has increased fears among young people that the military regime that took power last year after huge Gen Z demonstrations will be no better than the government it overthrew. Four Gen Z activists, Herizo Andriamanantena, Miora Rakotomalala, Dina Randrianarisoa and Nomena Ratsihorimanana, were arrested on 12 April, one of their lawyers said, two days after taking part in a protest calling for an election date to be set. Mandelson was fired nine months into the job over his friendship with Jeffrey Epstein.

The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: Charlize Theron joins chorus of disapproval over Timothée Chalamet’s ballet comments
events.description: The former ballet dancer said Chalamet’s comments were ‘reckless’ in an interview with the New York Times in which she also discussed her violent childhood Actor and former ballet dancer Charlize Theron has joined the chorus of disapproval aimed at Timothée Chalamet over his remarks that appeared to disrespect performers of ballet and opera . In an interview with the New York Times , Theron said: “Oh, boy, I hope I run into him one day,” adding: “That was a very reckless comment on two art forms that we need to lift up constantly because, yes, they do have a hard time. But in 10 years, AI is going to be able to do Timothée’s job, but it will not be able to replace a person on a stage dancing live.” Continue reading...

events.title: Canadian astronaut’s bon mots help heal wounds from French language row
events.description: Jeremy Hansen praised for speaking French in space after Air Canada chief’s linguistic snub exposed tensions and drew rebuke from PM Few people foresaw humanity’s quest for the moon as accurately as the 19th-century French author Jules Verne, whose two works –From the Earth to the Moon and Around the Moon – anticipated many of the features of modern lunar exploration. But Verne’s language had never been spoken in deep space until the Canadian astronaut Jeremy Hansen uttered four words during Nasa’s recent Artemis II mission. Continue reading...

events.title: Former VFL footballer Barry Cable cleared of historic child sex abuse charges
events.description: The 82-year-old was accused of abusing girls in the 1960s but was acquitted in Perth despite judge finding the alleged victim was probably telling the truth Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The former champion footballer Barry Cable has been acquitted of a slew of historical child sexual abuse charges despite a judge finding the alleged victim was likely telling the truth. The 82-year-old faced a judge-only criminal trial over allegations he abused a girl aged about eight or nine at his family home in the late 1960s. Continue reading...

events.title: U.S. seizes Iranian cargo ship. And, tariff refund portal launches
events.description: U.S. forces seized an Iranian-flagged cargo vessel in the Strait of Hormuz. And, an online government portal for processing tariff refunds launches today.

events.title: U.S. seizes Iranian cargo ship in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The U.S. has taken custody of an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz after firing on its engine room, President Trump said in a post on social media.

events.title: Bundesliga debut by female coach marred by misogyny
events.description: Marie-Louise Eta's historic debut as a head coach in the men's Bundesliga ended in defeat. While her appointment is a sign of progress, the reactions to it leave no doubt about how much is still to be done.

events.title: Bulgaria enters uncharted territory as Radev wins big
events.description: Preliminary results show that former President Rumen Radev has won Bulgaria's general election with a clear majority and the biggest result for a single party since 1997. Will Bulgaria finally get a stable government?

events.title: The German refugees who found shelter in Yugoslavia
events.description: After the Nazis seized power in 1933, many Germans escaped by fleeing abroad. Some ended up in what was then Yugoslavia. German historian Marie Janine Calic tells their story.

events.title: Bayern Munich beat Stuttgart to clinch Bundesliga title
events.description: Bayern Munich are German league champions for a record-extending 35th time after beating Stuttgart 4-2. They have now won 13 of the past 14 Bundesliga titles.

events.title: US tariff refund system launches for companies to claim billions in import taxes
events.description: The US Customs and Border Protection announced Monday that its refund portal for Trump's import tariffs – struck down by the US Supreme Court in mid-February – is now open to applications from businesses. The agency said in court filings that more than 330,000 importers paid a total of about $166 billion on over 53 million shipments.

events.title: US seizes Iranian-flagged cargo ship
events.description: The #US said it had seized an Iranian-flagged cargo ship that tried to get past its naval blockade near the Strait of #Hormuz on Sunday. #Iran vowed to retaliate, throwing planned peace talks between the two countries into question.
events.ticker_label: 💥 US seizes Iranian-flagged cargo ship

events.title: 🔴 Live: Lebanon names ex-ambassador to US Simon Karam to lead Israel talks
events.description: No description available.

events.title: IDF soldier damages Jesus statue with sledgehammer in Lebanon - as Netanyahu responds
events.description: Israel's prime minister Benjamin Netanyahu condemned "in the strongest terms" images of an Israeli soldier damaging a statue of Jesus.

events.title: Rebel Wilson appears in court for start of defamation trial over social media posts
events.description: Rebel Wilson has appeared in court for the start of a defamation trial brought against her by another actress who starred in her directorial debut.

events.title: Rat poison found in jars of baby food in Austria, police say
events.description: A baby food brand is recalling its products sold in Austrian SPAR markets after some tested positive for rat poison.

events.title: North Korea launches missiles in latest show of force
events.description: North Korea has been urged to "stop the provocative acts" after firing missiles from its east coast early on Sunday morning.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 North Korea launches missiles in latest show of force

events.title: Migrants rush to apply under Spain’s new mass legalization program
events.description: Spain has launched a mass legalization measure for migrants, allowing them to apply for legal status starting on Monday

events.title: Rat poison found in some baby food jars in Central Europe leads to recall
events.description: Countries across Central Europe have pulled HiPP baby food off shelves after rat poison was found in some jars over the weekend

events.title: Tired of political turmoil, Bulgarians give ex-president a convincing mandate for change
events.description: Bulgaria's center-left coalition led by ex-president Rumen Radev has won the parliamentary election, according to the central electoral commission

events.title: India and South Korea agree to nearly double trade to $50B by 2030
events.description: South Korea and India have agreed to nearly double bilateral trade to $50 billion by 2030, up from about $27 billion

events.title: British PM faces angry lawmakers over Mandelson's appointment as ambassador
events.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer faces angry lawmakers over Peter Mandelson's appointment as ambassador to Washington

events.title: Colombian President Petro vows a lawsuit after Ecuador's Noboa alleges ties to gang boss 'Fito'
events.description: Colombian President Gustavo Petro says he will sue Ecuadorian President Daniel Noboa

events.title: Exit poll suggests the center-left coalition of former president Rumen Radev will win Bulgaria election
events.description: Exit poll suggests the center-left coalition of former president Rumen Radev will win Bulgaria election

events.title: The Epstein files: Rights experts demand accountability, call for probe into trafficking allegations
events.description: UN independent human rights experts called on Thursday for justice and accountability for young women and girls who were trafficked systematically as part of allegations contained in the so-called Epstein files.

events.title: Student charged, weapon recovered after 3 teens targeted in incident linked to high school tradition: police
events.description: A high school student in Massachusetts faces assault charges after allegedly firing gel projectiles at teens not involved in a "Senior Assassin" game.

events.title: Hawaii tourist charged with attempted murder, accused of stabbing veteran boat captain on snorkel tour
events.description: Avery Nissen of Kansas allegedly tried to kill a boat captain during a three-hour snorkel tour in Hawaii and faces attempted murder charges, police say.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Hawaii tourist charged with attempted murder, accused of stabbing veteran boat captain on snorkel tour

events.title: Survey finds nearly one-third of Long Island residents say Jews should 'move on' from the Holocaust
events.description: A new survey of Long Islanders finds nearly one-third of residents believe Holocaust education should not be mandatory and Jewish people should "move on."

events.title: ‘Lego bandit’ accused in bizarre $34K scheme replacing minifigures with pasta
events.description: Jarrelle Augustine is accused of buying Lego sets from Target, removing valuable pieces and returning boxes stuffed with pasta, Irvine police say.

events.title: Trump’s favorite field marshal: Who is Pakistan’s powerful army chief Asim Munir with deep intel ties
events.description: How Pakistan's military chief Asim Munir became one of the few foreign figures trusted by both Trump and Iran's security establishment today.

events.title: Escaped wolf Neukgu returned to South Korean zoo after nine-day search involving thermal imaging drones
events.description: Neukgu, a 2-year-old wolf, escaped a South Korean zoo nine days ago and was safely captured after an extensive search involving drones and more.

events.title: Starmer and Macron accused of ‘playing at being relevant’ with Strait of Hormuz plan
events.description: Britain and France push a European-led naval mission to safeguard shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, separate from U.S. military operations in the region.
